title: UN to honour 2 Indian peacekeepers posthumously & award Major Abhilasha Barak
content: India's unwavering commitment to UN peacekeeping is highlighted as two brave soldiers, Lance Havildar Harbhajan Singh and Naib Subedar Sujit Kumar Pradhan, will be posthumously honored with the Dag Hammarskjold Medal. Major Abhilasha Barak, a pioneering combat helicopter pilot, also receives the Military Gender Advocate of the Year award for her exceptional work.

title: 'Rajat Patidar is still the same person he was before captaincy': Karthik
content: Dinesh Karthik, now an RCB mentor, reflects on the team's championship defense, highlighting Rajat Patidar's calm leadership and Devdutt Padikkal's evolving game. He shares insights on coaching international stars, adapting to their independence, and the crucial mindset for finishers. Karthik emphasises that team combinations, not just individual selection, define value.

title: Four lines that 'layoff email' from Meta Leadership has for those on H-1B visa
content: Meta has initiated another round of significant layoffs, impacting nearly 8,000 employees globally, with notifications starting in Singapore. While many are let go, over 7,000 will transition to AI initiatives. The company is providing immigration guidance and severance packages, including 16 weeks' pay plus two weeks per service year, and 18 months of COBRA coverage for affected staff.

title: IPL Qualifier 2: Can Gujarat Titans crack the Vaibhav Sooryavanshi code?
content: Qualifier 2 pits Gujarat Titans' disciplined bowling against Rajasthan Royals' explosive opener Vaibhav Sooryavanshi. After a tough outing against RCB, GT's pacers, including Siraj and Rabada, face the challenge of containing the teenager who has redefined the tournament's emotional tempo with his aggressive batting.

title: ‘God’s gift to Indian cricket’: Gavaskar’s massive Vaibhav Sooryavanshi verdict
content: Legendary Sunil Gavaskar hailed 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi as a "God's gift" after his IPL 2026 heroics, advocating for his immediate inclusion in India's T20 squad for England. Sooryavanshi's explosive 97 off 29 balls, featuring 12 sixes, shattered records and showcased his fearless, technically sound batting, prompting calls for his fast-tracking into international cricket.

title: What Sundar Pichai said on Google’s answer to Anthropic, OpenAI's AI coding agents
content: Google's CEO Sundar Pichai acknowledges a current lag in AI coding agents. He states competitors like Anthropic lead in complex developer tasks. Pichai expresses strong confidence that internal data and new model iterations will rapidly bridge this gap. Google is actively working to regain its position at the forefront of this crucial technology.

title: 'Drishyam 3' BO day 8: Mohanlal's mystery drama sees marginal dip
content: Mohanlal's 'Drishyam 3' continues its strong box office run, earning approximately Rs 6.50 crore on its eighth day. The film has amassed Rs 81.80 crore domestically, with Kerala leading contributions. Overseas collections push the global gross towards Rs 200 crore. Director Jeethu Joseph's final chapter in the thriller trilogy is a massive success.

title: Cloudflare CEO On AI Layoffs: Prince defends cutting measurers
content: Cloudflare is laying off over 1,100 employees, about 20% of its workforce, to restructure around AI adoption. CEO Matthew Prince explained the cuts target 'measurers' – roles like middle management and operations – as AI enhances efficiency. He believes AI will boost productivity for 'builders' and 'sellers', allowing greater investment in growth areas.

title: Claude Lemieux death rumors explained: What really happened to NHL legend?
content: Former NHL player Claude Lemieux has died at the age of 60. TMZ Sports reported that he may have died by suicide, though no official confirmation has been released publicly. Lemieux won four Stanley Cups during his legendary NHL career and became known as one of hockey’s fiercest playoff competitors. Fans are also remembering his emotional final appearance in Montreal earlier this week.

title: Blake Lively hits breaking point amid Taylor Swift-Travis Kelce fallout rumors: Report
content: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce have remained distant and private about their feelings for the Hollywood power couple, Blake Lively and Ryan Reynolds, as they gear up to get married in the next few weeks in New York City. As speculations swirl about whether Blake Lively and Ryan Reynolds will be attending Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s high profile wedding, it seems the actress has reached a breaking point in her life.

title: Claude Lemieux family post takes heartbreaking turn after emotional message resurfaces
content: Claude Lemieux’s death at age 60 has left the hockey world heartbroken. TMZ Sports and The Athletic reported that the NHL legend died by suicide. Fans also revisited an emotional old post from his son Brendan Lemieux after the news surfaced. The four-time Stanley Cup champion is being remembered for his legendary playoff career, fearless style, and unforgettable moments in NHL history.

title: ‘I had to stop him!’ Shastri reveals when Kohli nearly lost control in Australia
content: Former India coach Ravi Shastri intervened during the 2014-15 Melbourne Test, stopping Virat Kohli from a heated confrontation with Mitchell Johnson after the pacer struck him on the helmet. This intense rivalry, however, fueled Kohli's remarkable batting performance that series, where he amassed 692 runs, solidifying his leadership and dominance in Australia.

title: Cyprus’s desire to buy BrahMos, Kamikaze drones from India alarms Turkey
content: Cyprus is seeking to acquire BrahMos cruise missiles and kamikaze drones from India, a move that has alarmed Türkiye. This potential defense deal, possibly part of an EU program, could significantly alter the regional military balance in the Eastern Mediterranean. The acquisition follows a roadmap for bilateral defense cooperation between India and Cyprus.

title: Cancer survivor US climber summits Mt Everest in record 9 hours, 55 min
content: Tyler’s feat was the culmination of a two-year pursuit repeatedly interrupted by setbacks. Thursday’s ascent was Andrews’ fifth serious attempt at the record across two Everest seasons and his second within days.

title: BJP rejigs 4 state units: Jat Sikh Kewal Singh Dhillon made Punjab chief ahead of polls
content: The BJP has appointed Kewal Singh Dhillon as its Punjab president, a strategic move to appeal to the influential Jat Sikh community ahead of assembly polls. This appointment, alongside changes in Delhi, Haryana, and Tripura, signals the party's ambition to expand its voter base beyond urban Hindus in Punjab.

title: Adivasis demand halt to tiger safari push, evictions in south India forests
content: Indigenous communities in Karnataka, Kerala, and Tamil Nadu are protesting wildlife tourism and tiger reserve expansion. They accuse authorities and conservation groups of commercializing ancestral lands, evicting families, and ignoring their rights. A joint declaration demands an immediate halt to relocations and recognition of their land rights, calling forced conservation 'colonization'.

title: Sussanne Khan says she found love again with Arslan after divorce
content: Sussanne Khan and Arslan Goni have opened up about their relationship, love, family bond and life together. The couple, who have been together for nearly five years now, reflected on finding love unexpectedly, balancing their hectic careers and protecting the sanctity of their relationship from public scrutiny. Sussanne also opened up about how her sons, Hrehaan and Hredaan, gradually bonded with Arslan over the years.

title: Natural refusal or calculated move? Why Siddaramaiah said no to Rajya Sabha seat
content: Siddaramaiah rejected a Rajya Sabha offer, opting to remain in Karnataka politics after resigning as Chief Minister. This decision complicates the transition for successor DK Shivakumar, as Siddaramaiah retains influence and potential leverage. His move signals a long-term strategy to shape state politics and potentially influence future elections.

title: Gujarat student stabbed to death in Canada; family informed after 12 days
content: A young Indian student, Vidhi, living in Canada for higher studies and aiming for permanent residency, was found murdered in the Niagara region. She was reportedly killed in a knife attack, with her family in Gujarat informed nearly 12 days later. Canadian authorities have launched a murder investigation.

title: ‘Future is here’: Yousuf compares Sooryavanshi to young Afridi after IPL carnage
content: Fifteen-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's explosive 97 off 29 balls against Sunrisers Hyderabad in the IPL 2026 Eliminator has stunned the cricket world, drawing comparisons to Shahid Afridi. The Rajasthan Royals sensation shattered records for most runs by an uncapped batter and most sixes in a season, showcasing fearless batting that has bowlers on edge.

title: Caste survey: Siddaramaiah's parting shot for DKS, Rahul
content: Outgoing Karnataka CM Siddaramaiah's last-minute acceptance of the caste census report presents a political dilemma for successor DK Shivakumar. The report, potentially validating the strength of backward classes, could alienate dominant communities. This also challenges Rahul Gandhi's national push for caste data, risking accusations of hypocrisy if not acted upon.

title: Horoscope today: Daily astrological predictions for May 29
content: Horoscope Today: Read daily horoscope predictions for May 29, 2026. Know all about the astrological events and influences that will be affecting each of the 12 zodiac signs. Our astrologer has analyzed the movements of the planets and the alignment of the stars to bring you the most accurate and up-to-date horoscope predictions for the day ahead. Get ready to discover what the stars have in store for you today.

title: At least 12 injured in clashes between rival groups in Haryana’s Nuh after Eid prayers
content: Violence erupted in Haryana's Nuh district following Eid prayers, with clashes in two villages leaving at least 12 people injured.  Old disputes fueled the altercations in Nizampur and Singar villages, involving stone-pelting and the use of weapons. Police intervened to control the situations, with one critically injured individual transferred for advanced care.

title: Super Sukhoi programme: Modernising IAF's work horse fighter fleet
content: The Indian Air Force is significantly upgrading its Sukhoi-30MKI fleet, a massive Rs 63,000 crore project. This 'Super Sukhoi' initiative will enhance avionics, sensors, and mission systems, including a new indigenous AESA radar and AI-powered computers.

title: 'We don’t want what happened to PBKS': Jofra Archer keeps RR grounded
content: Jofra Archer's fiery spell dismantled Sunrisers Hyderabad in the IPL 2026 Eliminator, propelling Rajasthan Royals towards the final. Archer, pleased with the team's bowling effort, highlighted the importance of powerplay wickets.  A remarkable 97 off 29 balls by 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi set a formidable target, showcasing RR's strong form as they prepare to face Gujarat Titans.

title: Why Yamuna is now brownish narrow stream
content: In an alarming display of environmental decline, the Yamuna has retreated to a thin stream, allowing locals to stroll across its once-swelling banks. This yearly event, worsened by minimal water allocations from Haryana and extreme heat evaporation, has left the river ecologically bereft.

title: Twisha Sharma death case: CBI to produce Giribala Singh in court on Friday
content: Former judge Giribala Singh was arrested by the CBI in Bhopal following a seven-hour interrogation regarding Twisha Sharma's death and dowry harassment allegations. Her anticipatory bail was quashed by the Madhya Pradesh high court a day prior. The Supreme Court has also instructed both families to avoid media interviews for a fair investigation.

title: Gujarat’s Banni grasslands: A heap of broken images, where the sun beats
content: NTPC’s proposed solar project in Kachchh’s Banni grassland is pitting India’s renewable energy ambitions against one of Asia’s most fragile ecosystems and the Maldharis, who have depended on the land for centuries. Abhinay Deshpande reports on the area that holds the Chhari-Dhand wetland conservation reserve and a hill that is both a refuge and compass for the pastoralists

title: Indian Air Force likely to oversee transport of NEET-UG re-test papers
content: This measure follows the cancellation of the original examination after an alleged question paper leak

title: A heat crisis in Telangana that burns beyond the thermometer
content: As Telangana battles one of its harshest summers in recent years, the rising burden of suspected heatstroke deaths and worsening urban heat stress are exposing the growing human cost of extreme weather. Even as the State rolls out heat mitigation measures and advisories, questions remain over ground-level implementation and the true scale of heat-related fatalities. Siddharth Kumar Singh reports on how the extreme heat is reshaping life, labour and survival

title: Child trafficking gangs on prowl, target poverty-stricken women in Andhra
content: Child trafficking and baby-selling gangs are on the prowl in Andhra Pradesh, taking advantage of destitute families, who are willing to sell their babies due to poverty. Rajulapudi Srinivas reports about child trafficking gangs having widespread network in States like Gujarat, Maharashtra, Delhi, and Telangana

title: A ‘Work-Live-Play’ township vs. farmers
content: 9,640 acres of land spread across nine revenue and 17 non-revenue villages has been identified for developing the Greater Bengaluru Integrated Township Project, popularly known as the Bidadi Township Project. But are farmers on board? Chetan B.C. and Aditya Bharadwaj find out.

title: Right to trauma care of citizens integral part of right to life: Supreme Court
content: A Bench of Justices J.K. Maheshwari and A.S. Chandurkar also directed the States to furnish periodic compliance reports by organising monthly meetings and uploading the minutes on the concerned portals

title: Watch: Karnataka CM change: Siddaramaiah resigns, what’s next? | Above the Fold | 28.05.2026
content: From Siddaramaiah’s resignation and the Congress leadership transition in Karnataka to India-China talks over the LAC and the Madras High Court’s cow slaughter ban ahead of Bakrid, and more.

title: Pune Court frames charges in Nasrapur child murder case in 28 days
content: The accused’s lawyers opposed the framing of charges during his first production. The court, after verifying the evidence, framed charges against him

title: First Kuki village in Churachandpur resettled 3 years after conflict began
content: Only the males have returned to Khodang, a village near the “buffer zone”, almost where three districts meet

title: Chhattisgarh BJP MLA accused of assaulting government official
content: The Nayab Tehsildar alleged that Ramkumar Toppo, who represents Sitapur constituency, assaulted him on May 27 after accusing him of misbehaving with his relative

title: Indrakeeladri Ghat road repair works halted due to heatwave in Vijayawada
content: The works will resume after weather conditions become normal, say the authorities, adding that vehicles will be allowed on the Ghat Road as usual from May 29 morning

title: Revanth Reddy hails NTR as a leader beyond politics
content: Unveiling TDP founder’s statue, CM said NTR gave political power to downtrodden by abolishing ‘Patel Patwari’ system, and empowering women by making them equal partners in property share

title: Visakhapatnam Collector sets June 30 deadline for Bhogapuram airport roads
content: Ahead of the Alluri Sitarama Raju International Airport’s opening in early July, Collector M. Abhishikth Kishore asks departments to finish road and connectivity works to the Bhogapuram airport by June 30

title: Former AIADMK Minister Vellamandi Natarajan joins TVK
content: “After the election, the AIADMK split into two. All of this is not right. As far as the TVK is concerned, they have accepted all our leaders, including Periyar, Anna, MGR, and Jayalalithaa. That is why I have come and joined here,” he told The Hindu

title: India to host Myanmar President Hlaing, exiled govt. calls him a ‘terrorist junta leader’
content: President of Myanmar will begin his visit from Bodh Gaya where he is expected to offer prayers at the Maha Bodhi temple and meet resident Myanmar nationals and monks

title: Australian Deputy PM Richard Marles to visit India for defence talks
content: Marles is expected to review bilateral defence cooperation with Defence Minister Rajnath Singh and discuss ways to  strengthen strategic and military ties between India and Australia

title: Resolve water disputes through cooperation: Modi tells States
content: The Prime Minister made the remarks at the 51st PRAGATI meeting, where seven critical infrastructure projects across railways, power and road sectors covering nine States worth around ₹30,000 crore were reviewed

title: National Health Accounts figures indicate high burden of health care costs on people
content: As per the latest figures from the National Health Accounts Estimates for India 2022-23, out of pocket expenditure is nearly half of the current health expenditure. Experts flag continued high level of unregulated privatisation, leading to major inequities in access to healthcare.

title: No legal action if infiltrators go back on their own: Amit Shah
content: Union government committed to identifying and deporting every single infiltrator from the country, says Home Minister

title: Govt. plans security protocol for fishing harbours under CISF oversight
content: A senior government official says the CISF will design a security template and guide the local administration in managing the security protocol. There are 1,547 notified fish landing centres and fishing harbours in 13 coastal States and UTs

title: Preparation, not fear will combat El Nino: Agriculture Minister Chouhan
content: El Nino is not something to be feared but something that we must prepare for, says Minister at inauguration of the Kharif Conference in Delhi

title: Twisha death case: Retired judge Giribala Singh arrested by CBI
content: CBI officers arrived at Giribala Singh’s residence and carried out an investigation; the agency plans to confront Ms. Singh and her son Samarth Singh, Twisha’s husband, with each other’s statements

title: Go by value proposition TG offers, refrain from incentive shopping: Industries official
content: Commissioner/Director of Industries Nikhil Chakravarthi who was speaking at a conference on data centres also urged firms to look beyond Hyderabad, wanted them to help promote backward linkages to spur manufacturing

title: NHAI identifies 1600 kms for monetisation for FY 27
content: The identified projects are across Haryana, Jharkhand, Karnataka, Rajasthan, Tamil Nadu, Telangana, Uttar Pradesh, Bihar, and Maharashtra.

title: PM should have sacked Pradhan for ‘ruining the future of lakhs of students’: Rahul Gandhi
content: Mr. Gandhi asks whether any background check was conducted into the controversial company that won the contract for the CBSE OSM process, accuses Mr. Pradhan of being “complicit”

title: The Hindu, Galgotias University establish Centre for Excellence in Journalism
content: Select students of Galgotias University will gain access to internship opportunities with The Hindu’s editorial teams, collaborative publishing opportunities for visual communication projects, curated workshops, field visits, and structured interventions by media professionals.

title: Panel on ‘artificial’ demographic changes may consider need for law to tackle issue: Amit Shah
content: If illegal migrants leave on their own, BJP government in West Bengal will refrain from filing legal cases against them and facilitate their departure, says Home Minister

title: Id prayers shifted to Brigade Ground in Kolkata as Bengal govt. imposes fresh curbs on Bakrid celebrations
content: Morning namaz moved from Red Road to Brigade Parade Ground this year, while public animal slaughter and roadside festivities remain prohibited under the new rules of the BJP government in the State; Hindu cow owners complain of economic distress due to sluggish cattle sale.

title: Left parties plan ‘rasta roko’ on June 9 against hike in fuel prices
content: They accuse the Central and State governments of failing to reduce taxes and pursuing anti-people policies

title: Lord Govindaraja rides Hanumantha and Gaja vahanams on sixth day
content: Artistes performing Chekka Bhajana and Kolatam, and ‘Prabandha Goshti’ form a part of the grand processions

title: Centre extended valuable support for Amaravati and Polavaram projects, says A.P. CM
content: Mahanadu is a grand success as 16.78 lakh people have participated in the programme held in hybrid mode, says Naidu and assures that worthy cadres will be duly elevated

title: Israel, Russia Added To UN Blacklist On Sexual Violence In Conflicts
content: Israel and Russia are now included in the "list of parties credibly suspected of committing or being responsible for patterns of rape or other forms of sexual violence" in armed conflict on the agenda of the Security Council.

title: US Aircraft Destroyed In Iran's Bushehr Province: Report
content: Iran's state TV said on early on Friday that a US aircraft was destroyed in Iran's Jam governorate in Bushehr.

title: US Declares 2 Brazilian Drug Groups As Terrorist Organisations
content: The US State Department announced it will designate two Brazilian criminal groups as foreign terrorist organizations on Thursday.

title: US, Iran Made "A Lot Of Progress" Towards Deal: JD Vance
content: The United States and Iran have made good progress towards a ceasefire extension deal but President Donald Trump is not yet ready to approve it, US Vice President JD Vance said Thursday.

title: Case Filed Against Rajasthan Minister Gautam Dak For Abusing Cops
content: Case filed against Gautam Dak at Dungla Police Station in Chittorgarh after an audio clip purportedly showing him abusing policeman went into circulation online.

title: Austrian Man Jailed 15 Years For Plotting Attack On Taylor Swift Concert
content: An Austrian court on Thursday sentenced a 21-year-old who admitted planning a foiled Islamist attack on a Taylor Swift concert in Vienna in 2024 to 15 years in prison.

title: "Won't Tolerate": US Threatens To Sanction Ally Oman Over Hormuz Tolls
content: He said the United States would "not tolerate any effort to impose a tolling system in the Strait of Hormuz," a reference to Iranian proposals to do so as part of a potential agreement with Washington.

title: Trump Mixes Up Iran And Venezuela Two Days After 'Perfect' Health Report
content: The apparent mix-up came just a day after Trump declared that his annual medical examination had gone "perfectly".

title: US Investigates Woman Who Accused Trump Of Sexual Assault: Report
content: E Jean Carroll has said a flirtatious, chance encounter with Trump in 1996 at Bergdorf Goodman's Fifth Avenue store in Manhattan ended violently.

title: Weather Today Highlights: Strong Dust Storm, Rains Likely In Delhi Shortly, Says Weatherman
content: Weather Updates Today, Heatwave, IMD Alert LIVE Updates: Light to very light rain is expected during the afternoon and evening, accompanied by strong winds and squalls reaching speeds of 50-60 kmph.

title: BJP's Social Engineering In Punjab: Jat Sikh Face, Hindu Connect, Dalit Outreach
content: The BJP's move is being seen as part of a larger political gamble aimed at breaking the perception of the party as primarily a "Hindu party" in Punjab.

title: "Terrorist Attack": Man Stabs, Wounds 3 At Swiss Train Station
content: Witnesses described scenes of panic and confusion when the man suddenly began stabbing people at the main train station in Winterthur.

title: Terrorists Still Holed Up In J&K's Rajouri, Troops Fire At Suspected Hideouts
content: Drone surveillance during the last few days of the operation has detected the movement of two-three Pakistani terrorists, including a commander, deep within the forest.

title: Heatwave Pushes Tourists To Shimla, But No Traffic Jam Yet
content: Ahmedabad-based tourist Sunil Kumar said, "We had seen reels on social media showing huge traffic jams in Shimla, but our experience has been completely different. We did not face any traffic jam from Chandigarh to Shimla"

title: Kerala BJP Leader Slams Congress And CPM Over Protest Against Probe Agency
content: "Isn't maintenance of law and order the duty of the police when the ED is investigating the former chief minister's daughter?" Kerala BJP vice president and former DGP R Sreelekha said

title: "Trinamool Will Be Finished In A Few Days": Party MP's Explosive Claim
content: The latest leader to speak up is Trinamool Congress's Rajya Sabha MP Sukhendu Shekhar Roy, who, among other things, had predicted a disintegration of the party.

title: DK Shivakumar's Next Challenge: Overturn 4-Decade-Old Trend Within 2 Years
content: DK Shivakumar will evidently need to work in close coordination with Siddaramaiah; otherwise, despite holding the office of chief minister, he risks finding himself isolated

title: Retired IAS Officer Dies In Delhi House Fire, AC Blast Suspected: Cops
content: An 80-year-old retired IAS officer died while his son sustained injuries after a fire broke out at a house in south Delhi's Hauz Khas area, police said on Thursday.

title: SC on share in royal property: Only 'throne' goes to eldest son, wealth must be split
content: eldest male lineal descendant of erstwhile Maharaja can bequeathed as per the rule of primogeniture . but properties and royal assets have to be divided between legal heirs as per succession law . the supreme court brings the curtain down on litigation going on for the last 49 years .

title: No case if you exit voluntarily, Amit Shah tells Bangladesh illegals
content: BJP govt in Bengal would not press charges against illegal immigrants if they chose to return voluntarily . "we all want that those who entered illegally go back voluntarily," says amit Shah . he accuses the previous governor of allowing unchecked infiltration into west Bengal . the BJP has made detention centres, but will not file any cases against them .

title: TMC MP Kakoli Ghosh Dastidar accuses Kalyan Banerjee of 'verbal abuse' inside LS
content: Kakoli Ghosh Dastidar accuses fellow party MP of "verbally abusing" her inside Lok Sabha . the complaint underscores the deep churn within the TMC . it comes a day after the four-term BJP MP quit all her posts in the regional party .

title: Centre may use IAF planes to fly NEET retest papers, PM Modi monitoring security: Report
content: Manash Pratim Gohain is a seasoned journalist with over two decades at The Times of India, where he has built a rich body of work spanning education policy, politics, and governance. Renowned for his incisive coverage of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, accreditation reforms, and skilling initiatives, he has also reported on student politics, urban policy, and social movements. His political reportage—both reflective and news-driven—adds depth to his writing, bridging policy with public impact. Through his 2,500 articles and related outlets, he has emerged as a trusted voice in national discourse, particularly in linking education reform to broader societal change. Read More

content: A Journalist by profession, Sandeep Moudgal has an experience of 18 years in the field with Politics and Policy as forte. Has a Master's degree in Ancient History and Archaeology from Mysore University along with a PGDJ from the Asian College of Journalism. Read More

title: INDIA bloc to meet on June 6 to chart its way forward
content: the opposition alliance INDIA will meet on June 6 to take stock of the political situation in the country . there has been a growing feeling among opposition parties that the governing BJP is gaining ground . the meeting may be dominated by opposition about the SIR of the electoral rolls .

title: Bashir Badr, master Urdu poet of love and melancholy, passes away

title: 196 days, 7 countries, 23 ports: Cocaine ship’s Brazil-India journey
content: Ashish Chauhan is Assistant Editor with The Times of India, Ahmedabad, with over 15 years of experience in crime, legal, and political reporting. He covers human smuggling, cyber fraud, and caste violence, and has broken major stories on fake IPLs and exam rackets. A former PTI and Gujarat Samachar journalist, he focuses on investigative, impact-driven journalism. Read More

title: Integrate all emergency helplines into '112' in three months, says SC
content: the suprem court has directed states and UTs to integrate all emergency/ambulance helplines into one helpline . the top court said that the right to trauma care of citizens is an integral part of right to life . a bench of justices also directed them to establish a functional grievance redressal system .

title: K'taka leadership change: Calm goodbye from state’s most combative socialist Siddaramaiah
content: Manu Aiyappa has been with The Times of India for over two decades, currently serving as the Political Editor. His reporting focuses on politics, public policy, and in-depth features that aim to inform and engage a broad readership. Over the years, his work has been recognised by both the Press Club of Bangalore and the Karnataka Media Academy for its clarity, credibility, and relevance to public discourse. Read More
